title: The AI Application Spending Report: Where Startup Dollars Really Go
body: Analysis of AI application spending patterns across Mercury's customer base
Data period: June-August 2025
Source: Mercury database of over 200,000 customers

title: Report Overview
body: Horizontal vs. vertical company distribution in AI spending
Creative tools dominate the spending landscape
Individual adoption patterns and consumer crossover trends
Vertical applications split between human augmentation and AI employees

title: Horizontal vs. Vertical Split
body: Horizontal companies make up 60% of the list
Vertical companies represent 40% of the list
Analysis based on Mercury's database of over 200,000 customers during June-August 2025

title: Data Source and Scope
body: The analysis examined spending across Mercury's database of over 200,000 customers
Analysis period: June-August 2025
Comprehensive view of startup spending patterns in AI applications

title: Creative Tools Lead the Categories
body: Creative tools represent the largest single category with ten companies on the list
This category dominates AI application spending among all segments analyzed

title: Top Creative Tool Companies
body: Freepik ranks at #4 on the overall spending list
ElevenLabs ranks at #5 on the overall spending list
Both companies lead the creative tools category in startup spending

title: Vertical Application Breakdown
body: Of the seventeen vertical application companies on the list
Total vertical companies represent a distinct subset of the AI spending landscape

title: Human Augmentation Focus
body: Twelve vertical application companies focus on supercharging humans
This represents the majority approach among vertical AI applications
These tools enhance human capabilities rather than replace them

title: AI Employee Applications
body: Five vertical application companies aim to serve as AI employees
This represents the automation-focused segment of vertical applications
These solutions are designed to function as autonomous workers

title: Individual Adoption Dominance
body: Nearly 70% of companies can be adopted by individuals
Individual adoptability is a key characteristic of top-spending AI applications
Bottom-up adoption drives significant spending patterns

title: Consumer Market Crossover
body: 12 companies also appeared on the Consumer Top 100 list
Significant overlap between B2B startup spending and consumer markets
Dual-market presence indicates broad appeal across user segments

title: Individual Product Origins
body: 11 companies having started as individual products
Nearly all consumer-crossover companies originated with individual-focused offerings
Bottom-up adoption strategy proves successful for AI application companies

title: Key Trends Summary
body: Horizontal solutions (60%) outpace vertical solutions (40%) in spending
Creative tools lead all categories with ten companies
Individual adoption (nearly 70%) drives the market
Human augmentation (12 companies) preferred over AI employees (5 companies) in vertical applications
